heading: Designation of United States Center for SafeSport
label: 220541 Designation of United States Center for SafeSport
notes: References in TextThe date of the enactment of the Empowering Olympic, Paralympic, and Amateur Athletes Act of 2020, referred to in subsec. (i), is the date of enactment of , which was approved .Pub. L. 116–189Oct. 30, 2020

children.label: A
children.text: serve as the independent national safe sport organization and be recognized worldwide as the independent national safe sport organization for the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: exercise jurisdiction over the corporation and each national governing body with regard to safeguarding amateur athletes against abuse, including emotional, physical, and sexual abuse, in sports;

children.label: C
children.text: maintain an office for education and outreach that shall develop training, oversight practices, policies, and procedures to prevent the abuse, including emotional, physical, and sexual abuse, of amateur athletes participating in amateur athletic activities through national governing bodies;

children.label: D
children.text: maintain an office for response and resolution that shall establish mechanisms that allow for the reporting, investigation, and resolution, pursuant to subsection (c), of alleged sexual abuse in violation of the Center’s policies and procedures;

children.label: E
children.text: ensure that the mechanisms under subparagraph (D) provide fair notice and an opportunity to be heard and protect the privacy and safety of complainants;

children.label: i
children.text: ensure that the national governing bodies and the corporation implement and follow the policies and procedures developed by the Center to prevent and promptly report instances of abuse of amateur athletes, including emotional, physical, and sexual abuse; and

children.label: ii
children.text: establish mechanisms that allow for the reporting and investigation of alleged violations of such policies and procedures;

children.label: G
children.text: publish and maintain a publicly accessible internet website that contains a comprehensive list of adults who are barred by the Center; and

children.label: i
children.text: the provision of written notice of the allegations against the individual;

children.label: ii
children.text: a right to be represented by counsel or other advisor;

children.label: iii
children.text: an opportunity to be heard during the investigation;

children.label: iv
children.text: in a case in which a violation is found, a reasoned written decision by the Center; and

children.label: v
children.text: the ability to challenge, in a hearing or through arbitration, interim measures or sanctions imposed by the Center.

children.label: A
children.text: to preclude the Center from imposing interim measures or sanctions on an individual before an opportunity for a hearing or arbitration;

children.label: B
children.text: to require the Center to meet a burden of proof higher than the preponderance of the evidence;

children.label: C
children.text: to give rise to a claim under State law or to create a private right of action; or

children.label: D
children.text: to render the Center a state actor.

children.heading: .—Policies and Procedures
children.label: b .—Policies and Procedures
children.text: The policies and procedures developed under subsection (a)(1)(C) shall apply as though they were incorporated in and made a part of .section 220524 of this title

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Center may, in its discretion, utilize a neutral arbitration body and develop policies and procedures to resolve allegations of sexual abuse within its jurisdiction to determine the opportunity of any amateur athlete, coach, trainer, manager, administrator, or official, who is the subject of such an allegation, to participate in amateur athletic competition.

children.heading: .—Preservation of rights
children.label: 2 .—Preservation of rights
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed as altering, superseding, or otherwise affecting the right of an individual within the Center’s jurisdiction to pursue civil remedies through the courts for personal injuries arising from abuse in violation of the Center’s policies and procedures, nor shall the Center condition the participation of any such individual in a proceeding described in paragraph (1) upon an agreement not to pursue such civil remedies.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), an applicable entity shall not be liable for damages in any civil action for defamation, libel, slander, or damage to reputation arising out of any action or communication, if the action arises from the execution of the responsibilities or functions described in this section, section 220542, or section 220543.

children.heading: .—Exception
children.label: 2 .—Exception
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply in any action in which an applicable entity acted with actual malice, or provided information or took action not pursuant to this section, section 220542, or section 220543.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Any civil action brought in a State court against the Center relating to the responsibilities of the Center under this section, section 220542, or section 220543, shall be removed, on request by the Center, to the district court of the United States in the district in which the action was brought, and such district court shall have original jurisdiction over the action without regard to the amount in controversy or the citizenship of the parties involved.

children.heading: .—Rule of construction
children.label: B .—Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this chapter shall be construed to create a private right of action.

children.label: A
children.text: the Center;

children.label: B
children.text: a national governing body;

children.label: C
children.text: the corporation;

children.label: D
children.text: an amateur sports organization or other person sanctioned by a national governing body under section 220525;

children.label: E
children.text: an amateur sports organization reporting under section 220530;

children.label: F
children.text: any officer, employee, agent, or member of an entity described in subparagraph (A), (B), (C), (D), or (E); and

children.label: G
children.text: any individual participating in a proceeding pursuant to this section.
develop training materials for specific audiences, including coaches, trainers, doctors, young children, adolescents, adults, and individuals with disabilities; and
not less frequently than every 3 years, update such training materials.

children.heading: .—Prohibition with respect to former employees and board members
children.label: 1 .—Prohibition with respect to former employees and board members
children.text: A former employee or board member of the corporation or a national governing body shall not work or volunteer at the Center during the 2-year period beginning on the date on which the former employee or board member ceases employment with the corporation or national governing body.

children.heading: Athletes serving on board of directors of national governing body.—
children.label: 2 Athletes serving on board of directors of national governing body.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: An athlete serving on the board of directors of a national governing body who is not otherwise employed by the national governing body, may volunteer at, or serve in an advisory capacity to, the Center.

children.heading: .—Ineligibility for employment
children.label: B .—Ineligibility for employment
children.text: An athlete who has served on the board of directors of a national governing body shall not be eligible for employment at the Center during the 2-year period beginning on the date on which the athlete ceases to serve on such board of directors.

children.heading: .—Conflicts of interest
children.label: 3 .—Conflicts of interest
children.text: An executive or attorney for the Center shall be considered to have an inappropriate conflict of interest if the executive or attorney also represents the corporation or a national governing body.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The corporation and the national governing bodies shall not interfere in, or attempt to influence the outcome of, an investigation.

children.heading: .—Report
children.label: B .—Report
children.text: In the case of an attempt to interfere in, or influence the outcome of, an investigation, not later than 72 hours after such attempt, the Center shall submit to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Energy and Commerce and the Committee on the Judiciary of the House of Representatives a report describing the attempt.

children.label: I
children.text: shall be confidential; and

children.label: II
children.text: shall not be subject to discovery, subpoena, or any other means of legal compulsion in any civil action in which the Center is not a party to the action.

children.heading: .—Rule of construction
children.label: ii .—Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this subparagraph shall be construed to prohibit the Center from providing work product described in clause (i) to a law enforcement agency for the purpose of assisting in a criminal investigation.

children.heading: .—Fiscal year 2021
children.label: A .—Fiscal year 2021
children.text: On , the corporation shall make a mandatory payment of $20,000,000 to the Center for operating costs of the Center for fiscal year 2021.January 4, 2021

children.heading: .—Subsequent fiscal years
children.label: B .—Subsequent fiscal years
children.text: For fiscal year 2022 and each fiscal year thereafter, the corporation shall make a mandatory payment of $20,000,000 to the Center not later than the close of business on the first regular business day in January.

children.heading: .—Funds from national governing bodies
children.label: 2 .—Funds from national governing bodies
children.text: The corporation may use funds received from 1 or more national governing bodies to make a mandatory payment required by paragraph (1).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Center may file a lawsuit to compel payment under paragraph (1).

children.heading: .—Penalty
children.label: B .—Penalty
children.text: For each day of late or incomplete payment of a mandatory payment under paragraph (1) after January 1 of the applicable year, the Center shall be allowed to recover from the corporation an additional $20,000.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Amounts transferred to the Center by the corporation or a national governing body shall be used, in accordance with section 220503(15), primarily for the purpose of carrying out the duties and requirements under sections 220541 through 220543 with respect to the investigation and resolution of allegations of sexual misconduct, or other misconduct, made by amateur athletes.

children.label: I
children.text: not less than 50 percent shall be used for processing the investigation and resolution of allegations described in subparagraph (A); and

children.label: II
children.text: not more than 10 percent may be used for executive compensation of officers and directors of the Center.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: I .—In general
children.text: If, after the Center uses the amounts as allocated under clause (i), the Center does not use the entirety of the remaining amounts for the purpose described in subparagraph (A), the Center may retain not more than 25 percent of such amounts as reserve funds.

children.heading: .—Return of funds
children.label: II .—Return of funds
children.text: The Center shall return to the corporation and national governing bodies any amounts, proportional to the contributions of the corporation and national governing bodies, that remain after the retention described in subclause (I).

children.heading: .—Lobbying and fundraising
children.label: iii .—Lobbying and fundraising
children.text: Amounts made available to the Center under this paragraph may not be used for lobbying or fundraising expenses.

children.label: A
children.text: to assess compliance with policies and procedures developed under this subchapter; and

children.label: B
children.text: to ensure that consistent training relating to the prevention of child abuse is provided to all staff of the corporation and national governing bodies who are in regular contact with amateur athletes and members who are minors subject to parental consent.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Center may impose on the corporation or a national governing body a corrective measure to achieve compliance with the policies and procedures developed under this subchapter or the training requirement described in paragraph (1)(B).

children.heading: .—Inclusions
children.label: B .—Inclusions
children.text: A corrective measure imposed under subparagraph (A) may include the implementation of an athlete safety program or specific policies, additional compliance audits or training, and the imposition of a probationary period.

children.label: I
children.text: enforce any corrective measure required under subparagraph (A); and

children.label: II
children.text: report the status of enforcement with respect to a national governing body within a reasonable timeframe.

children.heading: .—Methods
children.label: ii .—Methods
children.text: The corporation may enforce a corrective measure through any means available to the corporation, including by withholding funds from a national governing body, limiting the participation of the national governing body in corporation events, and decertifying a national governing body.

children.heading: .—Effect of noncompliance
children.label: iii .—Effect of noncompliance
children.text: If the corporation fails to enforce a corrective measure within 72 hours of a request under clause (i), the Center may submit to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Energy and Commerce and the Committee on the Judiciary of the House of Representatives a report describing the noncompliance.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Not less frequently than annually, the Center shall submit to Congress a report on the findings of the audit under paragraph (1) for the preceding year and the status of any corrective measures imposed as a result of the audit.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: Each report under subparagraph (A) shall be made available to the public.

children.heading: .—Personally identifiable information
children.label: ii .—Personally identifiable information
children.text: A report made available to the public shall not include the personally identifiable information of any individual.
The number and nature of misconduct complaints referred to the Center, by sport.
The number and type of pending misconduct complaints under investigation by the Center.
The number of misconduct complaints for which an investigation was terminated or otherwise closed by the Center.
The number of such misconduct complaints reported to law enforcement agencies by the Center for further investigation.
The number of discretionary cases accepted or declined by the Center, by sport.
The average time required for resolution of such cases and misconduct complaints.

Information relating to the educational activities and trainings conducted by the office of education and outreach of the Center during the preceding quarter, including the number of educational activities and trainings developed and provided.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after the end of a fiscal year, the Comptroller General of the United States shall make available to the public a certification relating to the Center’s independence from the corporation.

children.label: A
children.text: A finding of whether a violation of a prohibition on employment of former employees or board members of the corporation under subsection (f) has occurred during the year preceding the certification.

children.label: B
children.text: A finding of whether an executive or attorney for the Center has had an inappropriate conflict of interest during that year.

children.label: C
children.text: A finding of whether the corporation has interfered in, or attempted to influence the outcome of, an investigation by the Center.

children.label: D
children.text: Any recommendations of the Comptroller General for resolving any potential risks to the Center’s independence from the corporation.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Comptroller General may take such reasonable steps as, in the view of the Comptroller General, are necessary to be fully informed about the operations of the corporation and the Center.

children.label: i
children.text: access to, and the right to make copies of, any and all nonprivileged books, records, accounts, correspondence, files, or other documents or electronic records, including emails, of officers, agents, and employees of the Center or the corporation; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the right to interview any officer, employee, agent, or consultant of the Center or the corporation.

children.heading: .—Treatment of privileged information
children.label: C .—Treatment of privileged information
children.text: If, under this subsection, the Comptroller General seeks access to information contained within privileged documents or materials in the possession of the Center or the corporation, the Center or the corporation, as the case may be, shall, to the maximum extent practicable, provide the Comptroller General with the information without compromising the applicable privilege.
